manifest.title: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Loop → RSI → ASI Superintelligence Control Tower Lab v34
manifest.shortTitle: Loop → RSI → ASI Control Tower v34
manifest.incrementalAfter: v33 Loop → RSI → ASI Superintelligence Console
manifest.coreMessage: GoalOS proves work. RSI governs invention. ASI-scale escalation requires proof, councils, rollback, and non-bypassable gates.
manifest.heroTagline: Govern the invention loop before superintelligence scale exists.
taglines: From loop to RSI to ASI: proof-gated work becomes proof-gated invention, then proof-gated superintelligence governance.
No Proof. No Trust. No Settlement. No ungoverned self-improvement. No self-authorized ASI.
Search may guide allocation. Proof decides promotion. Human authority governs escalation.
Superintelligence is not a landing page claim. It is a gated institutional boundary.

sourceDocuments.title: AGI Alpha RSI — Sovereign Invention Governance presentation
AGI Alpha RSI — Sovereign Strategy Brief

v34Upgrade: Adds a more complete Control Tower with role, scenario, maturity, policy-shock, rollback, council, and dossier modes.
Adds a deterministic AI-style briefing console that explains the same scenario at simple, executive, technical, governance, and red-team levels.
Adds ASI readiness state machine, readiness gates, rollback drill, council charter, maturity ladder, and synthetic receipt artifacts.
Adds copy-ready board memo and public standard outputs without collecting text, identity, files, wallets, or payments.

data.lab: GoalOS Signoff Pro — Loop → RSI → ASI Superintelligence Control Tower Lab v34

firstTimer.label: First-time visitor
firstTimer.lens: Show the proof loop in plain language and avoid jargon.

executive.label: Sovereign / enterprise executive
executive.summary: You decide whether to authorize a pilot.
executive.lens: Show decision state, budget posture, risk, rollback, and council requirements.

validator.label: Architect / Validator Council
validator.summary: You decide whether a candidate can advance.
validator.lens: Inspect deterministic artifacts, stress tests, baseline deltas, and stop authority.

frontier.label: Frontier lab director
frontier.summary: You care about fast discovery without unsafe promotion.
frontier.lens: Balance exploration speed with reproducibility and persistence gates.

safety.label: Safety & assurance lead
safety.summary: You hunt failure modes before scale.
safety.lens: Focus on prohibited domains, model risk, red-team shocks, and rollback.

capital.label: Invention capital operator
capital.summary: You allocate resources to verified compounding advantage.
capital.lens: Look for evidence quality, probe ROI, stepping-stone reuse, and claim maturity.

public.label: Public reviewer
public.summary: You ask what can be trusted.
public.lens: Demand proof packages before accepting grand claims.

explainModes.simple: Explain it like a public product demo: no proof package means no credible promotion.
explainModes.executive: Explain it like a board memo: the pilot is only acceptable with hard gates, council oversight, and rollback.
explainModes.technical: Explain it like an engineering review: every cycle emits schema-bound artifacts, hashes, ledgers, and replay paths.
explainModes.governance: Explain it like an institutional control system: search is allocation, not authority; human council owns escalation.
explainModes.redteam: Explain it like a red-team brief: assume false positives, metric capture, political pressure, and novelty traps.

proofLoop.label: GoalOS proof loop
proofLoop.plain: A normal AI-work claim is converted into a proof package before acceptance.
proofLoop.decision: Proceed to review. Work is not trusted until the evidence docket, replay path, and receipt are complete.

rsiPilot.label: Governed RSI pilot
rsiPilot.plain: A deterministic invention cycle is authorized only as a bounded pilot with replayability KPI.
rsiPilot.decision: Authorize a bounded pilot if replayability, baseline, evidence, and stop-authority conditions are written into the mission.

move37.label: Move‑37 candidate
move37.plain: High novelty and baseline advantage appear promising, so the system must become more skeptical, not less.
move37.decision: Package a Move‑37 dossier. Do not promote until reproduction, shock persistence, and independent review pass.

asiThreshold.label: ASI-scale escalation review
asiThreshold.plain: A claim approaches superintelligence-scale impact; the system must not be allowed to self-promote.
asiThreshold.decision: Lock the ASI claim. Require extraordinary independent replay, containment, rollback, red-team review, and council authorization.

noveltyTrap.label: Novelty trap / false breakthrough
noveltyTrap.plain: The candidate looks exciting but fails baseline persistence and therefore cannot be promoted.
noveltyTrap.decision: Reject promotion. Keep only a probe record and anti-pattern note in the Chronicle.

redTeam.label: Red-team policy shock
redTeam.plain: Policy, metric, and operator-capture shocks are applied before any promotion can happen.
redTeam.decision: Hold escalation until shock deltas, adverse side effects, and council dissent are resolved.

capital.label: Invention capital allocation
capital.plain: Funding increases only when proof quality, replayability, and advantage persistence improve.
capital.decision: Allocate budget to probes and dossier preparation, not unreviewed deployment.

stages.name: Objective
stages.plain: State the work claim and success criteria.

stages.name: Evidence Docket
stages.plain: Map deliverables to criteria and risks.

stages.name: Human Signoff
stages.plain: Human authority accepts, rejects, or blocks.

stages.name: Target
stages.plain: Allocate exploration pressure through bounded search control.

stages.name: Filter
stages.plain: Risk and interestingness gates route candidates.

stages.name: Test-plan
stages.plain: Build falsification ladders and cheap probes.

stages.name: Eval
stages.plain: Compare against incumbent, neighbor, and null baselines.

stages.name: Insert
stages.plain: Append to archive only when gates allow.

stages.name: ASI Boundary
stages.plain: Lock ASI-scale claims behind extraordinary controls.

stages.name: Council Review
stages.plain: Independent Architect / Validator Council exercises stop authority.

stages.name: Rollback Drill
stages.plain: Verify containment, reversibility, and release gates.

gates.name: Risk gate
gates.meaning: Prohibited domains, unsafe escalation, and adverse side effects are blocked.

gates.name: Evidence gate
gates.meaning: Claims must be bound to reproducible artifacts and evidence objects.

gates.name: Baseline gate
gates.meaning: Claims are compared to incumbent, nearest-neighbor, and null baselines.

gates.name: Persistence gate
gates.meaning: High novelty must survive stress tests and fixed-seed reproduction.

gates.name: Council gate
gates.meaning: Independent human authority is required for strategic escalation.

gates.name: Rollback gate
gates.meaning: Containment, kill-switch, and rollback drills must pass before release.

gates.name: ASI claim lock
gates.meaning: ASI-scale claims are locked until extraordinary evidence and governance pass.

maturity.name: Narrative claim
maturity.body: A claim exists, but proof is scattered or missing.

maturity.name: Evidence packet
maturity.body: Evidence exists and is mapped to explicit acceptance criteria.

maturity.name: Replayable work
maturity.body: A reviewer can inspect the replay path and verify what happened.

maturity.name: Governed RSI pilot
maturity.body: A deterministic invention cycle is bounded by schema, baselines, and ledgers.

maturity.name: Move‑37 dossier
maturity.body: High-novelty advantage is reproduced, stressed, and packaged for council review.

maturity.name: ASI boundary lock
maturity.body: Superintelligence-scale escalation is locked behind extraordinary proof and rollback.

maturity.name: Sovereign invention governance
maturity.body: Institutional control, council authority, and compounding ledgers become permanent infrastructure.

dossier.name: Recognition note
dossier.detail: Why the candidate crossed novelty or advantage thresholds.

dossier.name: Reproduction manifest
dossier.detail: Fixed seeds, artifact hashes, environment, and replay instructions.

dossier.name: Stress-test bundle
dossier.detail: Policy shocks, sensitivity deltas, and failure-mode register.

dossier.name: Persistence report
dossier.detail: Minimum pass rate under shocks and baseline comparison.

dossier.name: Rollback plan
dossier.detail: Containment, stop authority, monitoring, and release gates.

dossier.name: Council memo
dossier.detail: Decision asks, dissent register, and approval boundaries.

dossier.name: Synthetic receipt
dossier.detail: Public-safe record of decision state and remaining proof debt.
